meta.title: The Case of the Missing Pixel
meta.author: Grog Studio

look.text: Yesterday's coffee. In this office, 'yesterday' is a range.

else.text: Cold, bitter, three days old. Perfect.
My eyes sharpen. I can see the individual PIXELS now.
(Rae gained: PIXEL VISION. Try looking at the case board again.)

look.text: One pixel. Cyan. Slightly scared. The client will pay good money for this.

then.text: THE CASE OF THE MISSING PIXEL
then.sub: an imported-assets demo — art by a neural network, jokes hand-typed

then.text: The name's Tracer. Rae Tracer. I find things nobody else can render.
Tonight's case: this room is missing exactly one pixel. Client says it's load-bearing.
This whole office was commissioned from a neural network. Even ME. No, it doesn't hurt. Much.

look.text: Mahogany. Or a very confident JPEG of mahogany.

open.text: The drawer contains two things: nothing, and a receipt for the nothing.

look.text: My mug. The AI drew the steam, but the coffee inside is all mine. Tragically.

pickup.text: Evidence? No. Breakfast? Also no. But it's coming with me.

look.text: A perfect coffee ring. The only clean geometry in this office.
A banker's lamp. The bank foreclosed. I kept the ambiance.

use.text: It's already doing its job: making everything look like a flashback.

use the_pixel.text: Easy... easy... THERE. Right back in the framebuffer where you belong.
PIXEL 217,43 RESTORED. DISPLAY INTEGRITY: 100%. GRATITUDE: LOADING...

use the_pixel.sub: The pixel went home. The mug never did.

use the_pixel.text: Another case closed. In this town, every pixel matters.
Rae Tracer will return in: THE GRADIENT ALWAYS RINGS TWICE.

use the_pixel.sub: Imported art + drawn sprites, one engine — made with Grog Studio

use.text: It's showing one dead pixel on an otherwise perfect screen. My whole career, in one image.

look.text: A CRT the size of a small fridge. The dead pixel on screen is the CASE.

use.text: Case closed. Don't touch the exhibits.

look.text: Four drawers: A-F, G-N, O-U, and 'MISC VILLAIN'. That last one rattles.

open.text: Locked. The key is in another budget.

actions.text: Sticky notes, red string... wait. With pixel vision I can FOLLOW the string.
It all points to... the window blinds. Third slat. Something's glowing there.
The board did its job. The string can rest now.
Sticky notes connected with red string. The industry-standard IDE.
It's all a blur of clues. I need to sharpen my eyes somehow. Coffee-somehow.

use.text: You don't USE a case board. You commune with it.

look.text: Outside: two rectangles of neon and the existential void. Property values are up.

open.text: And let the neon in? This office runs on 40% gloom minimum.

look.text: There it is. The missing pixel, hiding in the blinds like a witness.

pickup.text: Gotcha. Come on, little guy. Let's get you home to the framebuffer.

look.text: Deleted assets. May they rest in cache.

use.text: I have nothing to throw away. Everything here is evidence or furniture.

look.text: My backup trench coat. Same silhouette, darker palette swap.

pickup.text: A detective without a spare coat is just a person with problems.

look.text: The glass says 'RAE TRACER, P.I.' backwards. From out there it says: hire me.

else.text: Leave? Mid-case? The pixel could be ANYWHERE in this room. Which is one room.
Also it's raining sprites out there. Hard pass.

walkto.text: Nobody leaves until the case is closed. Especially me. I live here.

look.text: It really ties the framebuffer together.

pull.text: I checked under it on day one. Just more JPEG.

look.text: Cold cases. Colder coffee stains. The stains have better documentation.

pickup.text: My filing system is 'geological'. Disturbing the strata would be reckless.

actions.text: I AM A JPEG OF A COMPUTER, RUNNING INSIDE A PNG OF AN OFFICE.
DO NOT THINK ABOUT IT TOO HARD. I DID. MY FAN HAS NOT STOPPED SINCE.

options.text: Any leads on the missing pixel?

actions.text: PIXEL 217,43 WAS LAST SEEN COMPLAINING ABOUT THE GLARE FROM THE WINDOW.
The window. Of course. Every runaway pixel goes toward the light.

options.text: How do you feel about the hand-drawn sprites next door?

actions.text: THE ENGINE ROOM DEMO? HAND-TYPED PIXELS. ARTISANAL. RUSTIC.
WE DO NOT SPEAK AT PARTIES. WE RENDER IN THE SAME ENGINE, THOUGH. IT IS VERY AWKWARD.
COOLNESS: MAXIMUM. GOODBYE, DETECTIVE. AVENGE OUR PIXEL.

look: In this light, everything looks like a clue. It isn't.
I've seen things you people wouldn't believe. This isn't one of them.
pickup: Not evidence. Not mine. Not today.
My coat pockets are full of older mysteries.
use: That's not how anything works, even in a point-and-click.
talk: It says nothing. The best witnesses always do.
open: Sealed tighter than the season two budget.
push: I push things for a living. Metaphorically. This one's literal, and no.
pull: The only thing I'm pulling tonight is an all-nighter.
